retrieved_docs.title: Clinical Laboratory -- Definition/Introduction
retrieved_docs.content: Optical sensor technologies: use  sensors that detect and measure various properties of a sample using light, such as refractive index or fluorescence, which helps identify and quantify various substances in biological fluids. [17]

retrieved_docs.title: Clinical Laboratory -- Definition/Introduction
retrieved_docs.content: Spectrophotometry is a technique used to measure the absorbance of colored compounds in solution, helping to identify and quantify various substances in blood and body fluids. [11]

retrieved_docs.title: Clinical Laboratory -- Definition/Introduction
retrieved_docs.content: Clinical laboratory specialists perform an array of tasks, including developing and validating new laboratory tests, assessing and defining the analytical and clinical performance, conveying laboratory results to clinicians, offering valuable education and guidance to the clinical team, evaluating the cost-effectiveness and intrinsic value, ensuring strict compliance with regulatory standards, engaging in quality assurance measures, and participating in both basic and clinical research endeavors. [24]

retrieved_docs.title: Clinical Laboratory -- Definition/Introduction
retrieved_docs.content: Flame emission photometry: a technique to measure the emission of light from a sample excited by a flame, helping to identify and quantify compounds in blood and body fluids. [14]

retrieved_docs.title: Clinical Laboratory -- Definition/Introduction
retrieved_docs.content: Clinical laboratory professionals have embraced technology over the years to derive answers to clinical questions. Modern clinical laboratories use technologies, including spectrophotometry, atomic absorption spectroscopy, cytometry, flame emission photometry, nephelometry, electrochemical, optical sensors, electrophoresis, and chromatography.

retrieved_docs.title: Clinical Laboratory -- Definition/Introduction
retrieved_docs.content: Electrophoresis is a technique to separate and analyze proteins in a sample, which helps diagnose and monitor conditions like multiple myeloma or amyotrophic lateral sclerosis. [18]

retrieved_docs.title: Clinical Laboratory -- Definition/Introduction
retrieved_docs.content: Chromatography is a technique that helps identify and quantify different components in blood and bodily fluids by separating and analyzing compounds in a sample according to their molecular properties, such as size, charge, or shape. [19]

retrieved_docs.title: Clinical Laboratory -- Definition/Introduction
retrieved_docs.content: Laboratory networks were developed across countries and states to foster coordination and collaboration within the specified geographic areas. [8] Quality management systems within these laboratories have recently become significant issues, including standardization of laboratory services, strengthening laboratory systems, and developing new and rapid diagnostic tools. These issues are continually addressed by local and international health authorities and technical experts employing a patient-centered approach.

retrieved_docs.title: Clinical Laboratory -- Definition/Introduction
retrieved_docs.content: Clinical laboratories perform testing logically and strictly. Generally, there are 3 phases of the laboratory testing process that each facility should follow. Standard operating procedure manuals and job aids are written for guidance for each phase step: pre-analytical, analytical, and post-analytical. [9] The pre-analytical phase is critical, with over 60% to 70% of laboratory errors occurring in this phase. [10]

retrieved_docs.content: Function General clinical laboratories provide standard diagnostic laboratory tests Specialty laboratories provide less commonly used diagnostic and confirmatory tests

retrieved_docs.title: Clinical Laboratory -- Definition/Introduction
retrieved_docs.content: Nephelometry is a technique to measure the turbidity of a solution, which helps diagnose and monitor conditions like liver disease or kidney failure. [15]

retrieved_docs.title: Clinical Laboratory -- Definition/Introduction
retrieved_docs.content: Automation has a wide-ranging impact, improving laboratory ordering, testing, and reporting processes while eliminating tedious and time-consuming chores. [22] It has ushered in a new era of heightened productivity by streamlining the use of reagents and materials, standardizing operations, and reducing the occurrence of outliers. The efficiency increases production rates and improves accuracy and precision in test results. Automation is a cornerstone in modern clinical laboratories, revolutionizing operations and elevating the overall quality of laboratory testing. [23]

retrieved_docs.title: UMLS -- Run 16 -- Level 3 -- Community 1976
retrieved_docs.content: The CDISC SDTM Laboratory Test Terminology by Code incorporates the concept “السيلنيوم (++Se)”, and this selenium test is classified as a type of “كيمياء غير محدد” (unspecified chemistry). This relationship indicates that the selenium laboratory measurement is part of a standardized terminology set while being grouped under a broader, non‑specific chemistry category.

retrieved_docs.title: UMLS -- Run 9 -- Level 4 -- Community 2851
retrieved_docs.content: The CDISC SDTM Laboratory Test Terminology by Name incorporates the laboratory test concept السيلنيوم (++Se). Within this terminology, السيلنيوم (++Se) is classified as a type of الكيمياء غير محدد, indicating that the selenium test is considered an unspecified chemistry assay. This categorization places the selenium measurement under the broader, non‑specific chemistry category in the CDISC SDTM framework, supporting standardized representation and analysis of laboratory data.

retrieved_docs.title: UMLS -- Run 11 -- Level 4 -- Community 2760
retrieved_docs.content: Allowed qualifier is a concept that applies to several medical substances and measurements. Chondroitin sulfáty and fluorodeoxyglukosa F18 are both designated as allowed qualifiers in the context of normy, indicating that normative reference ranges are established for these agents. Conversely, normy itself functions as an allowed qualifier when reporting levels of vitamin B 12 and when describing the use of orlistat, linking standard reference values to both a vitamin assay and a pharmacologic weight‑loss therapy. This interconnected framework ensures that appropriate reference standards are consistently applied across diverse diagnostic and therapeutic contexts.

retrieved_docs.title: UMLS -- Run 31 -- Level 3 -- Community 2450
retrieved_docs.content: Essential hypertension is a clinical condition that includes the standardized MedDRA query “ارتفاع ضغط الدم (SMQ)”. The Arabic term “ضغط الدم” is used to interpret essential hypertension, indicating that blood pressure measurement is central to its definition. Moreover, within the Newborn Screening Terminology, the concept “ضغط الدم” is listed as a member of a specific subset, emphasizing the relevance of blood‑pressure assessment in newborn screening protocols. This linkage of essential hypertension, its Arabic descriptors, and the inclusion of blood‑pressure concepts in newborn screening illustrates the importance of consistent terminology across disease classification systems and pediatric screening practices.

retrieved_docs.title: UMLS -- Run 11 -- Level 1 -- Community 228
retrieved_docs.content: Hemolytic jaundice and perinatal jaundice, also referred to by the Arabic term “يرقان وليدي,” can occur during the fetal and/or neonatal period and may be observed throughout the broader period of life between birth and death, including the specific stage of infancy. In clinical assessment, a total hemoglobin concentration that falls below the reference range is interpreted as indicative of hemolytic jaundice and perinatal jaundice, and the measurement of total hemoglobin concentration is used to interpret the presence of this condition. Recognizing the timing of occurrence, the laboratory interpretation of low hemoglobin, and the synonymous terminology enhances patient evaluation and care.

retrieved_docs.title: UMLS -- Run 11 -- Level 2 -- Community 757
retrieved_docs.content: The CDISC SDTM Laboratory Test Terminology, both in its “by Code” and “by Name” versions, incorporates several specific laboratory test concepts, including measurements of selenium (السيلنيوم (++Se)), estrogen (الإستروجين), and prostaglandins. Within this terminology, selenium and estrogen are each classified as types of “unspecified chemistry” (كيمياء غير محدد), indicating that they belong to a broader, non‑specific chemical testing category. This “unspecified chemistry” category itself is nested under a more detailed parent concept that pertains to combined folic acid and cyanocobalamin assessments (叶酸+氰钴胺素) recorded at particular time points in serum or whole‑blood specimens with a nominal measurement type. Additionally, the concept of selenium is explicitly linked to the general class “measurement of substance,” reinforcing its role as a specific analyte measurement within the laboratory test hierarchy. Together, these relationships illustrate how the CDISC SDTM framework organizes individual analyte tests—such as selenium, estrogen, and prostaglandin measurements—under layered categories that progress from broad chemical groupings to precise assay specifications, facilitating standardized reporting and interpretation of laboratory results in clinical research and patient care.

retrieved_docs.title: UMLS -- Run 3 -- Level 1 -- Community 105
retrieved_docs.content: Hemolytic jaundice and perinatal jaundice are conditions that typically occur in the fetal and/or neonatal period and are closely related to the Arabic‑language concept “يرقان وليدي” (neonatal jaundice). Evaluation of these jaundice types relies on the measurement of total hemoglobin concentration, which is used to interpret the clinical significance of the findings. In this context, a laboratory result that falls below the reference range is interpreted as an important indicator when assessing hemolytic jaundice and perinatal jaundice in newborns.

retrieved_docs.title: UMLS -- Run 4 -- Level 3 -- Community 1911
retrieved_docs.content: The CDISC SDTM Laboratory Test Terminology by Code incorporates the laboratory test concept “السيلنيوم (++Se)”, representing a selenium measurement within the standardized data model for clinical trial laboratory data.

retrieved_docs.title: UMLS -- Run 8 -- Level 3 -- Community 2096
retrieved_docs.content: The concept “Measurement of substance” is linked to the specific test “السيلنيوم (++Se)” through an inverse‑is‑a relationship, indicating that the selenium measurement is a particular type of substance measurement. The selenium measurement itself is further classified by another inverse‑is‑a relationship as belonging to the broader, though undefined, category “كيمياء غير محدد” (unspecified chemistry). This unspecified chemistry category is connected by a has‑parent relationship to the more detailed assay “叶酸+氰钴胺素:印象:时间点:血清/全血:名义型,” which represents a folic acid and cyanocobalamin test evaluated at a specific time point in serum or whole‑blood specimens. Together, these relationships place the selenium measurement within a hierarchical framework of chemical laboratory testing, linking it from a general measurement of substances to an undefined chemistry group and ultimately to a specific folate‑cobalamin assay context.

retrieved_docs.title: UMLS -- Run 1 -- Level 3 -- Community 1268
retrieved_docs.content: The CDISC SDTM Laboratory Test Terminology by Name incorporates Prostaglandin measurement as a concept within its subset, indicating that this specific laboratory test is included in the standardized terminology set used for clinical data interchange.

retrieved_docs.title: UMLS -- Run 10 -- Level 3 -- Community 1616
retrieved_docs.content: Newborn Screening Terminology encompasses the concept of تسمم الحمل, indicating that the terminology used for newborn screening includes this specific condition referenced in Arabic.

retrieved_docs.title: UMLS -- Run 17 -- Level 4 -- Community 2857
retrieved_docs.content: The Clinical Data Interchange Standards Consortium (CDISC) Terminology serves as a standardized vocabulary that incorporates a wide range of clinical concepts. Within this terminology, the concept “Bone Mineral Density Test” is included, providing a uniform definition for this diagnostic procedure. The CDISC Terminology also encompasses the Czech term “sexuální partneři” (sexual partners), allowing consistent capture of relationship information in patient histories. Additionally, the Arabic phrase “عدد خلايا الدم الحمراء” (red blood cell count) is part of the same terminology, ensuring that laboratory measurements can be recorded and exchanged accurately across language barriers. By integrating these diverse concepts—diagnostic tests, social history elements, and laboratory values—the CDISC Terminology facilitates interoperable, multilingual clinical data exchange and supports comprehensive patient care.

retrieved_docs.title: UMLS -- Run 0 -- Level 3 -- Community 2030
retrieved_docs.content: Vitamin D and analogues is a pharmacological class that encompasses both natural vitamin D2 (ergocalciferol) and synthetic compounds such as dihydrotachysterol; dihydrotachysterol is identified as a member of the Vitamin D and analogues group, and vitamin D2 is likewise included within this broader category. This classification highlights the relationship between the natural form of vitamin D2 and its analogues, informing clinicians about the spectrum of agents that share similar biological activity and can be considered when selecting vitamin D–related therapies.

retrieved_docs.title: UMLS -- Run 23 -- Level 3 -- Community 1550
retrieved_docs.content: Prostaglandins, total measurement is identified as a SPECIFIC SUBSTANCE CHEMISTRY TESTS, indicating that it is a specialized chemical assay. This SPECIFIC SUBSTANCE CHEMISTRY TESTS is placed under the broader category غازات الدم الشرياني, which is the Arabic term for arterial blood gases. The term arterial blood gases classifies غازات الدم الشرياني, linking the English and Arabic nomenclature. Consequently, the total measurement of prostaglandins is understood as a specific substance chemistry test that falls within the overall arterial blood gases testing framework.

retrieved_docs.title: UMLS -- Run 24 -- Level 2 -- Community 717
retrieved_docs.content: The CDISC SDTM Laboratory Test Terminology, both in its “by Code” and “by Name” versions, incorporates the laboratory test concepts السيلنيوم (++Se) (Selenium) and الإستروجين (Estrogen) as included items in their respective subsets. Both السيلنيوم (++Se) and الإستروجين are classified under the broader category كيمياء غير محدد (unspecified chemistry), indicating that they are considered specific chemical analytes within an undefined chemical class. This unspecified chemistry category itself is a child of the more complex laboratory test concept 叶酸+氰钴胺素:印象:时间点:血清/全血:名义型 (folic acid + cyanocobalamin impression at a specific time point in serum/whole blood, nominal type), situating Selenium and Estrogen within a hierarchical framework of related biochemical measurements. In addition, a Selenium supplement is linked to the السيلنيوم (++Se) concept, reflecting the clinical relevance of Selenium supplementation. The “by Name” subset of the CDISC SDTM terminology also contains a Prostaglandin measurement concept, further illustrating the breadth of analytes covered by this standardized laboratory test terminology.

retrieved_docs.title: UMLS -- Run 2 -- Level 4 -- Community 2921
retrieved_docs.content: The CDISC SDTM Laboratory Test Terminology by Code and the CDISC SDTM Laboratory Test Terminology by Name both incorporate the laboratory test concepts السيلنيوم (++Se) and الإستروجين, ensuring that these selenium and estrogen measurements are represented in both the coded and named subsets of the CDISC SDTM laboratory test terminology for consistent clinical data capture and analysis.
